steps.title: Identify the difference of squares pattern
steps.content: The expression $x^2 - 100y^2$ can be rewritten as $(x)^2 - (10y)^2$ since $100y^2 = (10y)^2$. This is a difference of squares in the form $a^2 - b^2$ with $a = x$ and $b = 10y$.

steps.title: Apply the difference of squares formula
steps.content:
Using the difference of squares formula with $a = x$ and $b = 10y$:

$$x^2 - 100y^2 = (x - 10y)(x + 10y)$$

Multiplying by the coefficient 11:

$$11(x^2 - 100y^2) = 11(x - 10y)(x + 10y)$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
